title: galactia flaviflora
intro: Galactia flaviflora, also known as the yellow milk pea or yellow milk vine, is a perennial climber in the pea family, native to the southeastern United States and the Caribbean. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Galactia flaviflora can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
